label: Simple chalazion without vision or orbital red flags
inclusion_criteria: Adult with clinician diagnosis or discharge impression of simple chalazion.
No vision change, severe eye pain, orbital cellulitis concern, eye trauma, contact lens complication, infection concern, recurrent mass, or specialist-directed eye plan.
exclusion_criteria: Vision change, severe eye pain, photophobia, pain with eye movement, orbital or periorbital cellulitis concern, or eye trauma.
Contact lens complication, recurrent or persistent eyelid mass, immune compromise, systemic illness, pediatric pathway, or specialist-directed ophthalmology plan.
Medication selection not supplied by clinician.
default_follow_up: Follow up with primary care, urgent care, optometry, or ophthalmology if symptoms are not improving, recur, or affect vision.

source_audit.notes: MedlinePlus and AAO support chalazion/eyelid-bump framing, conservative warm-compress care, avoiding squeezing or popping, and escalation for vision trouble, worsening pain/swelling, infection concern, or persistent/recurrent eyelid mass. Autonomous Phase 1651-1700 approved this narrow v1 only for clinician-diagnosed simple chalazion without vision change, severe eye pain, orbital/periorbital cellulitis concern, eye trauma, contact lens complication, recurrent/persistent mass, immunocompromised/pediatric pathway, systemic illness, or specialist-directed eye plan.

review.notes: Reviewed as a narrow production ontology phenotype. MedlinePlus and AAO support chalazion/eyelid-bump framing, conservative warm-compress care, avoiding squeezing or popping, and escalation for vision trouble, worsening pain/swelling, infection concern, or persistent/recurrent eyelid mass. Autonomous Phase 1651-1700 approved this narrow v1 only for clinician-diagnosed simple chalazion without vision change, severe eye pain, orbital/periorbital cellulitis concern, eye trauma, contact lens complication, recurrent/persistent mass, immunocompromised/pediatric pathway, systemic illness, or specialist-directed eye plan.
required_modifiers: adult with clinician diagnosis or discharge impression of simple chalazion
no vision change, severe eye pain, eye trauma, or orbital/periorbital cellulitis concern documented
no contact lens complication, recurrent/persistent mass, immunocompromised, pediatric, systemic illness, or specialist-directed eye pathway
